documentation.readme: The Metasploit Framework is an open-source tool released under a BSD-style license. For detailed licensing information, refer to the COPYING file.
usage_sections: Join our GitHub Discussions for community support and general questions
Join the Metasploit Slack for real-time chat
Submit GitHub Issues for bug reports and feature requests
Follow @metasploit on X or @metasploit@infosec.exchange on Mastodon for updates
Visit the Using Metasploit section of the documentation.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for metasploit-framework (rapid7/metasploit-framework). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.name: run
function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for metasploit-framework. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with rapid7/metasploit-payloads (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with MobSF/Mobile-Security-Framework-MobSF (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with 0xInfection/TIDoS-Framework (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the exploit category. Fits when the engagement needs: The Metasploit Framework is an open-source tool released under a BSD-style license. For detailed licensing information, refer to the COPYING file.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: The Metasploit Framework is an open-source tool released under a BSD-style license. For detailed licensing information, refer to the COPYING file.
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `metasploit-framework` from its upstream repository, then run `metasploit-framework --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. The Metasploit Framework is an open-source tool released under a BSD-style license. For detailed licensing information, refer to the COPYING file.

name: metasploit-framework
summary: The Metasploit Framework is an open-source tool released under a BSD-style license. For detailed licensing information, refer to the COPYING file.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need exploit, use metasploit-framework because The Metasploit Framework is an open-source tool released under a BSD-style license. For detailed licensing information, refer to the COPYING file.
When chaining exploit tools, pair metasploit-framework with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `metasploit-framework --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
